description:
Evaluation of skills/spec-led-bootstrap (as of the evidence-ledger-split branch, 39d0295) against a real brownfield adoption (Atlas: 186 subjects, 210 decisions, ~8 weeks in). Verdict: the skeleton is strong — state-detecting, idempotent, defer-by-default extraction, resume with bw-vs-worktree consistency halt — but it is tuned to prevent the wrong failures. It guards against over-extraction and premature lockdown; the things that actually hurt the reference adoption (paraphrase specs, containerized runtimes, orphaned CI, stalled graduation) pass through untouched. One outright bug.

Items in priority order; each could split into its own child if this becomes an epic.

## 1. BUG: nonexistent finding codes in reference docs

references/detection-matrix.md and references/configurable-levers.md use `append_only/modal_downgraded` and `overlap/requirement_overlap`. Real codes (per branch_check.ex resolver defaults) are `append_only/must_downgraded` and `overlap/must_stem_collision`. A bootstrap following these docs writes phase6 severity overrides that never match and classifies conformance findings against codes that never fire — silently, since unknown config keys do not error loudly.

## 2. CI has no rung on the ladder

Detection reads CI signals (1.4) and phase4 touches CI for cover.test, but no ticket ever installs the `mix spec.check` gate itself. phase6 done-criteria assume it is "the rightmost gate in CI" — installed by whom? Also `mix spec.review` is never mentioned in the skill, yet the PR review artifact is the most immediately visible adoption value. Proposal: fold CI wiring into the phase2 ticket — run `spec.check --base <pr-base>`, render + upload the spec.review artifact, and (post evidence-ledger) fetch the `spec-evidence` ref read-only with the unauthenticated-attestation caveat.

## 3. Finish evidence-ledger integration (csg epic re-touched only ~13 lines here)

Missing: `mix spec.evidence.install_hook` in phase0 done-criteria; `spec.sync` in the scaffolded daily-loop guidance; and a detection signal for legacy adopters — `git ls-files .spec/state.json` non-empty should classify as pre-ledger workspace and emit a migration ticket (`mix spec.evidence.migrate`, untrack state.json, install hook). Every pre-csg adopter hits this path; the matrix currently reads a stale tier and marches on.

## 4. phase1 self-contradiction manufactures paraphrase specs

SKILL.md Phase 3.3 says drafts get "one starter requirement per public function the candidate exports" — the exact recipe for inventory-flavored specs (reference adoption: cli.spec.md ~70% code restatement), and something subject-extraction.md's own anti-patterns section forbids when no @doc backs the claim. Align: default placeholder is "Bootstrap draft — behavior to be specified in phase2 review."; per-function requirements only where a @doc makes a real behavioral claim. Add a phase2 merge-gate line: "no placeholder requirements survive promotion to status: active" — otherwise drafts graduate with scaffolding inside and the corpus starts life as a module inventory with "shall" sprinkled on.

## 5. Ask the containerized-runtime question at detection time

Biggest tribal-knowledge friction in the reference adoption: host/container split (git-sensitive spec.prime/spec.next on host; DB-backed command verifications in the container). Bootstrap never asks. One detection signal (docker-compose.yml / Dockerfile.dev present + ecto in deps) and one template parameter — split <verify_cmd> into <host_check_cmd> and <container_verify_cmd>, and write the split loop into the scaffolded .spec/AGENTS.md.

## 6. Emit a deferred "graduation review" ticket

Staged adoption's observed failure mode: severities parked at :warning/:info "until the corpus is clean" and the graduation date recedes forever. Bootstrap already has the tool: `bw defer`. When the epic stops below phase6, emit one deferred ticket ("Graduation review: run mix spec.status; graduate codes that stayed clean, or write explicit :off opt-outs"), deferred ~4 weeks. Converts "graduate later" from an intention into a scheduled artifact.

## 7. Propagate the two-armed ADR rubric into the scaffold (see specled_-4kg)

configurable-levers.md already has bootstrap document Spec-Drift trailers in the generated .spec/AGENTS.md; extend to the ADR fork (durable/cross-cutting -> ADR; ticket-local -> trailer with a one-line reason) and put the same rubric in the scaffolded decisions/README.md. Reference adoption produced 210 decision files in ~8 weeks (~1/3 commit-grade) because the scaffold shipped without the rubric; bootstrap is the one moment to set the norm before the corpus exists.

## 8. Consistency sweep (small)

- argument-hint offers phase0|phase1|phase2|phase4|phase6 but the ladder defines 7 phases; if 3/5 are deliberately excluded as recommended stops, say so.
- adoption-phases.md phase3 uses branch_guard_requirement_without_test_tag where the validator-side sibling is requirement_without_test_tag; docs use them interchangeably — disambiguate.
- Umbrella advice says "unless v1.1 of SpecLedEx is in the dep" — version reference will rot; tie to a capability probe instead.

## Keep as-is

Detect/target/extract/emit shape; resume consistency halt; defer-by-default with unmapped_change as the organic re-prompt; the anti-patterns section; ":info is a debugging mode, not an adoption stance."

## Rationale

This skill determines whether the next repo's corpus becomes the reference adoption at its best (incident-derived invariants, 93% same-commit spec sync) or at its worst (inventory specs, parked severities, filler ADRs). Items 4, 6, 7 are the steering ones — they encode the adoption lessons the ladder does not yet know about.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-123-spec-led-bootstrap-skill - implement
Log: (interactive session)

Commit: e672e81 on specled_-123/bootstrap-gaps (worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-bootstrap-gaps)
Files touched: skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md, references/detection-matrix.md, references/configurable-levers.md, references/adoption-phases.md, references/task-templates.md, references/subject-extraction.md
Tests added: none (docs-only ticket; no code paths changed)
Verification stubs flipped: none
Verification tier: change-scoped — mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix format --check-formatted, mix spec.next, mix spec.check --base origin/main --no-run-commands (exit 0); full-system proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1

Summary: All 8 ticket items landed. The finding-code bug (item 1) is fixed against the verified resolver defaults in branch_check.ex (append_only/must_downgraded, overlap/must_stem_collision), with an added warning that misspelled severity keys silently never match. CI wiring folded into phase2 (spec.check --base + spec.review artifact from the scaffolded workflow template, spec-evidence fetched read-only with the unauthenticated caveat); evidence-ledger integration completed (install_hook + spec.sync in phase0, pre-ledger detection via tracked state.json emitting a spec.evidence.migrate ticket template); phase1 placeholder-spec recipe replaced with the single Bootstrap-draft placeholder + @doc-backed exceptions and a phase2 no-placeholder promotion gate; runtime-split detection (1.6) with host_check_cmd/container_verify_cmd tokens; deferred graduation-review ticket (bw defer ~4 weeks) whenever target < phase6; two-armed ADR rubric propagated to scaffolded AGENTS.md + decisions/README.md; consistency sweep done (argument-hint phase coverage, requirement_without_test_tag vs branch_guard_ sibling disambiguated everywhere incl. a wrong dangling-tag claim in detection-matrix, umbrella advice tied to a capability probe). File-touch guard finding on specled.package surface handled via Spec-Drift trailer (docs-only, no package contract change).

title: spec-led-bootstrap skill: fix finding-code bug + close the adoption gaps a real brownfield adoption exposed
